tool_name: Surface Roughness Conversion — Ra, Rz, Rq, Rt & Machining Process Capability

inputs.name: Arithmetic Mean Roughness (Ra)
inputs.note: ISO 4287 §4.2.1 — Ra: most universal specification parameter; measured by profilometer
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Machining / Finishing Process
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Evaluation Cut-off Length (λ_c)
inputs.note: ISO 4288:1996 Table 1 — standard cut-off selection based on expected Ra range
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: ISO Surface Finish N-Number (N1–N12)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Measurement Traverse Length (L_t)
inputs.note: ISO 4288: L_t = 5 × λ_c for standard measurement (5 sampling lengths)

formulas: Rz_um = Ra_um * 7.2  // [µm] — Rz (Ten-Point Height / Maximum Height) from Ra — Ref: ISO 4287 — empirical ratio Rz ≈ 7.2 × Ra for turned and ground surfaces (range 4–10×); ISO 1302:2002 §4.3
Rq_um = Ra_um * 1.11  // [µm] — Rq (Root Mean Square Roughness) from Ra — Ref: Rq ≈ 1.11 × Ra for Gaussian height distribution — ISO 4287 §4.2.2; used in tribology and bearing contact analysis

Rt_um = Ra_um * 10  // [µm] — Rt (Total Profile Height — Maximum Peak to Valley) from Ra — Ref: Rt ≈ 10 × Ra (conservative estimate for machined surfaces) — Whitehouse 'Surfaces and their Measurement' §3
Ra_from_N_um = 0.025 * Math.pow(2, N_value - 1)  // [µm] — ISO N-Number Ra Equivalence (from N-number input) — Ref: ISO 1302:2002 Table 1 — Ra_N = 0.025 × 2^(N−1); N7 = 1.6 µm; N9 = 6.3 µm

Ra_microinch = Ra_um * 39.37; Rz_microinch = Rz_um * 39.37  // [µin] — ASME B46.1 CLA (Centreline Average) — US Microinch Conversion — Ref: 1 µm = 39.37 µin — ASME B46.1-2019 §1.1; CLA (US) = Ra (ISO)

const PR = {grinding:[0.1,1.6], turning_finish:[0.8,3.2], turning_rough:[3.2,12.5], milling_finish:[0.8,3.2], milling_rough:[3.2,12.5], honing:[0.05,0.4], lapping:[0.01,0.2], edm:[0.4,6.3], shot_blast:[6.3,25]}; const r=PR[machining_process]; Ra_min_proc=r[0]; Ra_max_proc=r[1]; in_range = Ra_um >= Ra_min_proc && Ra_um <= Ra_max_proc  // [µm, µm, boolean] — Achievable Ra Range & Process Capability Assessment — Ref: Process capability ranges — Kalpakjian Manufacturing Engineering 7th Ed. §26; ISO 286-2 surface finish correlation

rules.message: Specified Ra is outside the normal capability of the selected machining process — verify process selection or accept that special tooling, passes, and conditions are required
Ra < 0.05 µm with standard machining: only achievable by lapping, superfinishing, or polishing — specify correct process
Traverse length < 5 × cut-off length: ISO 4288 requires minimum 5 sampling lengths for reliable Ra measurement — increase traverse length

smart_warnings.message: Ra < 0.4 µm from EDM: requires mirror EDM with very low energy settings — expect 5–10× longer machining time vs standard EDM at Ra 1.6 µm
Ra inconsistency: specified Ra > 3.2 µm but N-value requests ≤ 1.6 µm — review which parameter is the actual requirement; Ra from profilometer takes precedence
High Rz on finish turning: check insert nose condition and cutting parameters — Rz > 25 µm suggests chatter or built-up edge; increase Vc or reduce feed
Very fine surface with long cut-off: ISO 4288 requires λ_c = 0.25 mm for Ra < 0.1 µm — using 0.8 mm cut-off will include waviness in Ra and give falsely high reading
N-number and Ra values are inconsistent by more than 2×: specify one parameter and derive the other — do not independently specify both to avoid conflicting drawing requirements

fmea.effect: Specified Ra is outside the normal capability of the selected machining process — verify process selection or accept that special tooling, passes, and conditions are required
Ra < 0.05 µm with standard machining: only achievable by lapping, superfinishing, or polishing — specify correct process
Traverse length < 5 × cut-off length: ISO 4288 requires minimum 5 sampling lengths for reliable Ra measurement — increase traverse length

fmea.failureMode: Ra < 0.4 µm from EDM: requires mirror EDM with very low energy settings — expect 5–10× longer machining time vs standard EDM at Ra 1.6 µm
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um < 0.4 && machining_process === 'edm'

fmea.failureMode: Ra inconsistency: specified Ra > 3.2 µm but N-value requests ≤ 1.6 µm — review which parameter is the actual requirement; Ra from profilometer takes precedence
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um > 3.2 && N_value <= 7

fmea.failureMode: High Rz on finish turning: check insert nose condition and cutting parameters — Rz > 25 µm suggests chatter or built-up edge; increase Vc or reduce feed
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Rz_um > 25 && machining_process === 'turning_finish'

fmea.failureMode: Very fine surface with long cut-off: ISO 4288 requires λ_c = 0.25 mm for Ra < 0.1 µm — using 0.8 mm cut-off will include waviness in Ra and give falsely high reading
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_um < 0.2 && cutoff_lambda_c_mm > 0.8

fmea.failureMode: N-number and Ra values are inconsistent by more than 2×: specify one parameter and derive the other — do not independently specify both to avoid conflicting drawing requirements
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: Ra_from_N_um > Ra_um * 2 || Ra_from_N_um < Ra_um * 0.5
